title: Stalemate & Draws
summary: Half a point is sometimes a win

steps.title: Stalemate
steps.text: Stalemate happens when the side to move has NO legal moves but their king is NOT in check. It's a draw. Beware: when you have an overwhelming advantage, take care not to stalemate the enemy king — it throws away the win!

steps.title: Other Ways to Draw
steps.text: Besides stalemate: (1) threefold repetition — same position reached three times; (2) 50-move rule — 50 moves without a capture or pawn move; (3) insufficient material — e.g. two kings alone, or king + knight vs king; (4) both players agreeing to a draw.

steps.title: Avoiding Accidents
steps.text: Even when you're obviously losing, stalemate can save you! Always check: does my opponent have a legal move? If not, and you're not in check, it's a draw. This is why endgame technique matters — even with K+Q vs K, careless play can stalemate.
